effects.desc: 3 charges (recharge 1d3 at dawn). On a hit, expend 1 charge: 10d6+40 necrotic damage and target makes DC 15 CON save or has disadvantage on STR and CON checks for 1 hour.
You command respect of those who share your faith and can perform religious ceremonies. You and companions can receive free healing at a temple.
Fellow guild members provide lodging and food if necessary. Guild connections grant access to powerful political contacts.
You have a second identity with documentation, acquaintances, and disguises. You can forge documents as long as you have seen an example.
You have a reliable contact who acts as liaison to a criminal network. You know messengers, caravan masters, and sailors who deliver messages.
You can always find a place to perform. Strangers who recognize you in a town where you performed typically take a liking to you.
When foraging for food, you find twice as much food as you normally would.
You can easily find the local city guard outpost, and officials cooperate with you if at all reasonable.
Excellent memory for maps and geography. You can find food and fresh water for yourself and up to five others each day.
Your hermitage gave you access to a unique discovery — a great truth about the cosmos, deities, or forces of nature.
You can earn 1 GP per day running a trading business or doing small jobs. You know trade routes and can estimate value of goods.
Welcome in high society; people assume you have the right to be wherever you are. Common folk make every effort to accommodate you.
If you don't know a piece of lore, you know where and from whom to obtain it — library, scriptorium, or sage.
You can secure free passage on a sailing ship for yourself and companions, assisting the crew in return.
You can quickly and accurately copy written text and recognize forgeries. You have connections with scholars providing library access.
Soldiers of your former organization recognize your authority. You can invoke rank to influence soldiers and requisition equipment.
You can move through crowds without drawing attention and find short-term shelter in a city without spending money.
You hurl a bubble of acid. One creature you can see within range must succeed on a DEX save or take 2d6 acid damage. Damage increases to 3d6 at level 5, 4d6 at level 11, 5d6 at level 17.
Until the spell ends, when you are hit by a weapon attack, the attacker takes 1d6 Force damage, and you gain Resistance to that attack's damage. Scales with level.

You brandish the weapon used in the spell's casting and make a melee attack with it. On a hit, the target suffers the attack's normal effects plus 1d8 Thunder damage if it moves before the start of your next turn. This thunder damage increases to 2d8 at level 5, 3d8 at level 11, and 4d8 at level 17.

You touch a creature, which must succeed on a CON save or take 1d10 Necrotic damage and lose the ability to regain HP until the start of your next turn. Undead have disadvantage on this save. Damage increases: 2d10 at 5th, 3d10 at 11th, 4d10 at 17th.
You create up to four torch-sized lights within range. You can combine them into one Medium glowing form or keep them separate. As a Bonus Action, you can move the lights up to 60 feet.

Create a tiny, harmless sensory effect that predicts weather; make a flower bloom or seed sprout; create an instantaneous sensory effect (rustling leaves, wind, crackling fire); light or snuff out a candle, torch, or small campfire.

A beam of crackling energy strikes one creature. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 1d10 Force damage. The spell creates more than one beam when you reach higher levels: two beams at 5th, three at 11th, and four at 17th.
You hurl a mote of fire at a creature or object. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 1d10 Fire damage. Damage increases: 2d10 at 5th, 3d10 at 11th, 4d10 at 17th.

For the duration, you have advantage on all Charisma checks directed at one creature of your choice that isn't hostile toward you. When the spell ends, the creature realizes that you used magic to influence its mood and becomes hostile toward you.

You brandish the weapon used in the spell's casting and make a melee attack with it against one creature within 5 feet of you. On a hit, the target suffers the weapon attack's normal effects, and you can cause green fire to leap from the target to a different creature of your choice that you can see within 5 feet of it. The damage increases at levels 5, 11, and 17.
You touch a willing creature. Once before the spell ends, the target can roll 1d4 and add the number rolled to one ability check, saving throw, or attack roll. Then the spell ends.

You touch one Large or smaller object. It sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet. The effect lasts for 1 hour or until you dismiss it. If the object is held or carried by a hostile creature, that creature must succeed on a DEX save.

A spectral hand appears at a point you choose within range. The hand lasts for 1 minute or until you dismiss it. The hand can manipulate objects, open unlocked doors and containers, stow or retrieve items from open containers, and pour contents from vials.

This spell repairs a single break or tear in an object. As long as the break or tear is no larger than 1 foot in any dimension, you mend it, leaving no trace of the former damage. This spell can physically repair a magic item or construct.
You point your finger toward a creature within range and whisper a message. The target (and only the target) hears the message and can reply in a whisper that only you can hear.

You drive a disorienting spike of psychic energy into the mind of one creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on an Intelligence saving throw or take 1d6 psychic damage and subtract 1d4 from the next saving throw it makes before the end of your next turn. The damage increases by 1d6 at levels 5, 11, and 17.
You create a sound or an image of an object within range that lasts for the duration. A creature that succeeds on an INT (Investigation) check against your spell save DC discerns it as an illusion.
You spray toxic mist at one creature within range. The target must succeed on a CON save or take 2d12 Poison damage. Damage increases: 3d12 at 5th, 4d12 at 11th, 5d12 at 17th.

This spell is a minor magical trick. You can create one of the following effects: a small sensory effect, light or snuff a fire, clean or soil an object, chill/warm/flavor non-living material, color or mark an object, create a trinket. Up to three effects can be active at a time.

A flickering flame appears in your hand. It sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet. The flame doesn't burn you. You can hurl it at a target within 30 feet as a ranged spell attack, dealing 1d8 fire damage on a hit. The damage increases by 1d8 at levels 5, 11, and 17.

A ray of freezing air and ice streaks toward a creature. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, it takes 1d8 Cold damage and its Speed decreases by 10 ft until the start of your next turn. Damage: 2d8 at 5th, 3d8 at 11th, 4d8 at 17th.

You touch one willing creature. Once before the spell ends, the target can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to one saving throw of its choice. It can roll the die before or after making the saving throw.
Flame-like radiance descends on a creature you can see. The creature must succeed on a DEX save or take 1d8 Radiant damage. Damage increases: 2d8 at 5th, 3d8 at 11th, 4d8 at 17th.

The wood of a club or a quarterstaff you are holding is imbued with nature's power. For the duration, you can use your WIS modifier for attacks and damage rolls with that weapon, and its damage die becomes a d8. The weapon also becomes magical.

Lightning springs from your hand. Make a melee spell attack against a creature. You have advantage if it's wearing metal armor. On a hit, it takes 1d8 Lightning damage and can't take reactions until the start of its next turn. Damage: 2d8 at 5th, 3d8 at 11th, 4d8 at 17th.
Choose a creature within range that has 0 HP and is not dead. The creature becomes Stable. In 5.2, this cantrip has a range of 15 feet instead of Touch.

You launch a mote of light at one creature or object within range. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 1d8 Radiant damage, and until the end of your next turn, it emits Dim Light in a 10-foot radius and can't benefit from being Invisible. Damage: 2d8 at 5th, 3d8 at 11th, 4d8 at 17th.

You create a momentary circle of spectral blades that sweep around you. All other creatures within 5 feet of you must each succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 1d6 force damage. The damage increases by 1d6 at levels 5, 11, and 17.

You manifest a minor wonder: amplify your voice, cause flames to flicker, cause tremors, create an instantaneous sound, open or slam doors, or alter the appearance of your eyes. Up to three effects can be active at a time.

You create a burst of thunderous sound that can be heard up to 100 feet away. Each creature other than you within 5 feet of you must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 1d6 thunder damage. The damage increases by 1d6 at levels 5, 11, and 17.

A mournful bell tolls around one creature you can see. It must succeed on a WIS save or take 1d8 Necrotic damage (1d12 if it's missing any HP). Damage increases at 5th (2d8/2d12), 11th (3d8/3d12), 17th (4d8/4d12).

Guided by a flash of magical insight, you make one attack with the weapon used in the spell's casting. The attack uses your spellcasting ability for the attack and damage rolls instead of STR or DEX. On a hit, the weapon deals 1d6 additional damage of its type. Damage: 2d6 at 5th, 3d6 at 11th, 4d6 at 17th.

You unleash a string of insults laced with magic. The creature must succeed on a WIS save or take 1d6 Psychic damage and have Disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn. Damage: 2d6 at 5th, 3d6 at 11th, 4d6 at 17th.

You utter a divine word, and burning radiance erupts from you. Each creature of your choice that you can see within range must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take 1d6 radiant damage. The damage increases by 1d6 at levels 5, 11, and 17.

You set an alarm against unwanted intrusion. Choose a door, a window, or an area within range no larger than a 20-foot cube. Until the spell ends, an alarm alerts you whenever a Tiny or larger creature touches or enters the warded area.

This spell lets you convince a beast that you mean it no harm. Choose a beast that you can see within range. It must have an Intelligence of 3 or less. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you for the spell's duration. If you or one of your companions harms the target, the spell ends.
Up to three creatures you can see within range must succeed on a CHA save or, whenever they make an attack roll or saving throw, they must roll a 1d4 and subtract the result.
Up to three willing creatures gain a 1d4 bonus to attack rolls and saving throws for the duration. At higher levels, one additional creature per slot above 1st.

Thin sheets of flames shoot from your outstretched hands. Each creature in a 15-foot cone must make a DEX save. On a fail, 3d6 Fire damage; half on success. Flammable objects ignite. +1d6 per slot above 1st.
One humanoid you can see must succeed on a WIS save or be Charmed by you. While charmed, it regards you as a friendly acquaintance. At higher levels: +1 target per slot above 1st.

You hurl a 4-inch-diameter sphere of energy at a creature that you can see within range. You choose acid, cold, fire, lightning, poison, or thunder for the type of orb you create, and then make a ranged spell attack against the target. If the attack hits, the creature takes 3d8 damage of the type you chose. At higher levels, the damage increases by 1d8 per slot level above 1st.

A dazzling array of flashing, colored light springs from your hand. Roll 6d10; the total is how many hit points of creatures this spell can affect. Creatures in a 15-foot cone that have the fewest hit points are affected first: they are blinded until the end of your next turn.
You speak a one-word command to a creature you can see. It must succeed on a WIS save or follow the command on its next turn. At higher levels: +1 target per slot above 1st.

For the duration, you understand the literal meaning of any spoken language you hear. You also understand any written language you see, but you must be touching the surface on which the words are written.

You either create or destroy water. Create Water: You create up to 10 gallons of clean water within range in an open container. Alternatively, the water falls as rain in a 30-foot cube within range, extinguishing exposed flames in the area. Destroy Water: You destroy up to 10 gallons of water in an open container within range.
A creature you touch regains 2d8 + your spellcasting ability modifier HP. This spell has no effect on undead or constructs. At higher levels: +2d8 per slot above 1st.

For the duration, you know if there is an aberration, celestial, elemental, fey, fiend, or undead within 30 feet of you, as well as where the creature is located. Similarly, you know if there is a place or object within 30 feet that has been magically consecrated or desecrated.

For the duration, you sense the presence of magic within 30 feet. If you sense magic, you can use your action to see a faint aura around visible magical creatures and objects, and learn their school of magic.

For the duration, you can sense the presence and location of poisons, poisonous creatures, and diseases within 30 feet of you. You also identify the kind of poison, poisonous creature, or disease in each case.
Your prayer empowers you with divine radiance. Until the spell ends, your weapon attacks deal an extra 1d4 Radiant damage on a hit.

The next time you hit a creature with a weapon attack before this spell ends, a writhing mass of thorny vegetation appears and grasps the target. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be restrained by the magical plants. A restrained target takes 1d6 piercing damage at the start of each of its turns.

Grasping weeds and vines sprout from the ground in a 20-foot square starting from a point within range. For the duration, these plants turn the area into difficult terrain. A creature in the area when you cast the spell must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be restrained by the entangling plants.
This spell allows you to move at an incredible pace. For the duration, your Speed increases by 10 feet, and taking the Dash action is a Bonus Action for you.

Each object in a 20-foot cube within range is outlined in blue, green, or violet light. Any attack roll against an affected creature or object has advantage, and it can't benefit from being invisible.

Bolstering yourself with a necromantic facsimile of life, you gain 1d4+4 temporary hit points for the duration. At higher levels, you gain 5 additional temporary hit points for each spell slot level above 1st.
Choose up to five falling creatures within range. Their rate of descent slows to 60 feet per round until the spell ends. They take no falling damage and can land on their feet.

You gain the service of a familiar, a spirit in the form of an animal you choose. The familiar acts independently, but it always obeys your commands. You can communicate telepathically and share senses with it.

You create a 20-foot-radius sphere of fog centered on a point within range. The sphere spreads around corners, and its area is heavily obscured. It lasts for the duration or until a wind of moderate or greater speed disperses it. At higher levels, radius increases by 20 ft per slot above 1st.

Up to ten berries appear in your hand, infused with magic for 24 hours. A creature can use its action to eat one, regaining 1 HP, and is nourished for a day. The berries lose potency if not consumed within 24 hours.

Slick grease covers the ground in a 10-foot square centered on a point within range. The area becomes Difficult Terrain for the duration. Each creature in the area when the spell is cast must succeed on a DEX save or fall Prone.

A flash of light streaks toward a creature. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 4d6 Radiant damage, and the next attack against it before the end of your next turn has advantage. +1d6 per slot above 1st.
A creature of your choice within range regains 2d4 + your spellcasting ability modifier HP. This spell has no effect on undead or constructs. At higher levels: +2d4 per slot above 1st.

When you are damaged by a creature within range, you can use your reaction to unleash a burst of flames. The creature must make a DEX save or take 2d10 Fire damage, or half on success. At higher levels: +1d10 per slot above 1st.

A willing creature you touch is imbued with bravery. Until the spell ends, the creature is immune to the Frightened condition and gains Temporary HP equal to your spellcasting ability modifier at the start of each of its turns. At higher levels: +1 target per slot above 1st.

You choose a creature you can see and mystically mark it as your quarry. Until the spell ends, you deal an extra 1d6 damage to the target whenever you hit it with an attack roll, and you have advantage on Perception and Survival checks to find it. If it dies, you can move the mark (no action). At higher levels: 8 hours (3rd), 24 hours (5th).

You choose one object that you must touch throughout the casting. If it is a magic item or some other magic-imbued object, you learn its properties, how to use them, and any charges it has. You also learn whether any spells are affecting the item and what they are.

You write on parchment, paper, or some other suitable writing material and imbue it with a potent illusion. To you and any creatures you designate when you cast the spell, the writing appears normal, written in your hand, and conveys whatever meaning you intended. To all others, the writing appears as if it were written in an unknown or magical script.
Make a melee spell attack against a creature. On a hit, the target takes 2d10 Necrotic damage. At higher levels: +2d10 per slot above 1st.
You touch a willing creature. Once on each of its turns until the spell ends, the target can jump up to 30 feet by spending 10 feet of movement.
You touch a creature. Its speed increases by 10 feet until the spell ends. At higher levels: +1 target per slot above 1st.
You touch a willing creature who isn't wearing armor. Its AC equals 13 + its DEX modifier. The spell ends if the target dons armor or dismisses the spell.

You create three glowing darts of magical force. Each dart automatically hits a target of your choice within range for 1d4 + 1 Force damage. The darts strike simultaneously. At higher levels: +1 dart per slot above 1st.

Until the spell ends, one willing creature you touch is protected against aberrations, celestials, elementals, fey, fiends, and undead. The protection grants advantage on saving throws against them, they can't possess or charm the target, and they have disadvantage on attack rolls against the target.

A ray of sickening greenish energy lashes out. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 2d8 Poison damage and must make a CON save or be Poisoned until the end of your next turn. +1d8 per slot above 1st.

An invisible barrier of magical force appears and protects you until the start of your next turn. You gain +5 to AC, including against the triggering attack, and you take no damage from Magic Missile.
A shimmering field appears around a creature within range, granting it +2 bonus to AC for the duration.

You create the image of an object, a creature, or some other visible phenomenon in a 15-foot cube within range. The image appears lifelike but has no physical presence. A creature that succeeds on an INT (Investigation) check against your spell save DC discerns it as an illusion.

You send creatures into a magical slumber. Roll 5d8. One creature of your choice within range whose current HP is less than or equal to the roll falls Unconscious until the spell ends, the sleeper takes damage, or someone uses an action to wake the sleeper. At higher levels: +2d8 per slot above 1st.

You gain the ability to comprehend and verbally communicate with beasts for the duration. The knowledge and awareness of many beasts is limited by their intelligence, but at minimum, a beast can give you information about nearby locations and monsters, including whatever it can perceive or has perceived within the past day.

A creature of your choice that you can see within range perceives everything as hilariously funny and falls into fits of laughter if this spell affects it. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or fall prone, becoming incapacitated and unable to stand up for the duration. A creature with an Intelligence score of 4 or less isn't affected.

The first time you hit with a melee weapon attack during this spell's duration, your weapon rings with thunder. The attack deals an extra 2d6 thunder damage to the target, which must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed 10 feet away from you and knocked prone.

A wave of thunderous force sweeps out from you. Each creature in a 15-foot cube must make a CON save. On a fail, it takes 2d8 Thunder damage and is pushed 10 feet away. On a success, half damage, no push. Unsecured objects are automatically pushed. At higher levels: +1d8 per slot above 1st.

This spell creates an invisible, mindless, shapeless, Medium force that performs simple tasks at your command until the spell ends. The servant springs into existence in an unoccupied space on the ground within range. It has AC 10, 1 hit point, and a Strength of 2.

A beam of crackling, blue energy lances at a creature, forming a sustained arc of lightning between you and the target. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes 2d12 Lightning damage. On each of your turns, you can use a Bonus Action to deal 1d12 Lightning damage to the target automatically. At higher levels: +1d12 initial damage per slot above 1st.

The next time you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack before this spell ends, your attack deals an extra 1d6 Psychic damage. Additionally, if the target is a creature, it must succeed on a WIS save or be Frightened of you until the spell ends.
Your spell bolsters up to three creatures within range. Each target's HP maximum and current HP increase by 5 for the duration. At higher levels: +5 HP per slot above 2nd.

By casting gem-inlaid sticks, rolling dragon bones, laying out ornate cards, or employing some other divining tool, you receive an omen from an otherworldly entity about the results of a specific course of action that you plan to take within the next 30 minutes. The DM chooses from the following possible omens: Weal, Woe, Weal and woe, or Nothing.
You touch a willing creature. Until the spell ends, the target's skin has a rough, bark-like appearance, and the target's AC can't be less than 16, regardless of what kind of armor it is wearing.

You can blind or deafen a foe. Choose one creature, which must make a CON save or be Blinded or Deafened. It can save again at the end of each of its turns. At higher levels: +1 target per slot above 2nd.

Your body becomes blurred, shifting and wavering. For the duration, any creature that targets you with an attack has disadvantage on the attack roll. An attacker immune to the blinded condition is unaffected.

You attempt to suppress strong emotions in a group. Each humanoid in a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on a point you choose must make a CHA save. On a fail, choose one effect: suppress the Charmed or Frightened condition, or make them indifferent about creatures of your choice that they are hostile toward.

You fill the air in a 5-foot cube with spinning daggers. A creature takes 4d4 Slashing damage when it enters the spell's area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there. At higher levels: +2d4 per slot above 2nd.

A flame, equivalent in brightness to a torch, springs forth from an object that you touch. The effect looks like a regular flame, but it creates no heat and doesn't use oxygen. A continual flame can be covered or hidden but not smothered or quenched.

You plant four pieces of nonmagical ammunition—arrows or crossbow bolts—in the ground within range and lay magic upon them to protect an area. Until the spell ends, whenever a creature other than you comes within 30 feet of the ammunition for the first time on a turn, it must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 1d6 piercing damage.

One humanoid you can see must succeed on a WIS save or become Charmed for the duration. The charmed target must use its action before moving to make a melee attack against one creature other than itself that you mentally choose.

Magical darkness spreads from a point you choose within range to fill a 15-foot-radius sphere for the duration. The darkness spreads around corners. Darkvision can't penetrate this darkness, and no nonmagical light can illuminate it.
You touch a willing creature. For the duration, that creature has Darkvision out to a range of 60 feet.

For the duration, you can read the thoughts of certain creatures. When you cast the spell and as your action on each turn until the spell ends, you can focus your mind on any one creature that you can see within 30 feet of you. If the creature you choose has an Intelligence of 3 or lower or doesn't speak any language, the creature is unaffected.

You touch one willing creature and imbue it with the power to spew magical energy from its mouth, provided it has one. Choose acid, cold, fire, lightning, or poison. Until the spell ends, the creature can use an action to exhale energy of the chosen type in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 3d6 damage on a failed save.

You touch a creature and bestow upon it a magical enhancement. Choose one effect: Bull's Strength (+1d4 STR checks, carry weight doubled), Cat's Grace (+1d4 DEX checks, no fall damage under 20 ft), Bear's Endurance (+1d4 CON checks, 2d6 temp HP), Fox's Cunning (+1d4 INT checks), Owl's Wisdom (+1d4 WIS checks), or Eagle's Splendor (+1d4 CHA checks). At higher levels: +1 target per slot above 2nd.

You sense the presence of any trap within range that is within line of sight. A trap, for the purpose of this spell, includes anything that would inflict a sudden or unexpected effect if a creature interacts with it. You learn the general nature of the danger presented by a trap you sense.

You evoke a fiery blade in your free hand. The blade is a simple melee weapon that you are proficient with. On a hit, it deals 3d6 Fire damage and emits bright light in a 10-foot radius. You can dismiss it as a Bonus Action. At higher levels: +1d6 per two slot levels above 2nd.

A 5-foot-diameter sphere of fire appears in an unoccupied space. Any creature that ends its turn within 5 feet must make a DEX save or take 2d6 Fire damage (half on success). As a Bonus Action, you can move the sphere up to 30 feet. At higher levels: +1d6 per slot above 2nd.

You touch a corpse or other remains. For the duration, the target is protected from decay and can't become undead. The spell also effectively extends the time limit on raising the target from the dead, since days spent under the influence of this spell don't count against the time limit of spells such as raise dead.

A line of strong wind 60 feet long and 10 feet wide blasts from you in a direction you choose for the spell's duration. Each creature that starts its turn in the line must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed 15 feet away from you in a direction following the line.

Choose a manufactured metal object. The object glows red-hot, and any creature in physical contact with it takes 2d8 Fire damage at the start of its turn and must succeed on a CON save or drop the object. At higher levels: +1d8 per slot above 2nd.

Choose a humanoid that you can see within range. The target must succeed on a WIS save or be Paralyzed for the duration. At the end of each of its turns, the target can make another WIS save. At higher levels: +1 target per slot above 2nd.

A creature you touch becomes Invisible until the spell ends. Anything the target is wearing or carrying is also invisible. The spell ends if the target attacks or casts a spell. At higher levels: +1 target per slot above 2nd.
Choose an object within range that is locked. A lock opens, a set of manacles unlocks, or a door barred from the other side becomes unbarred. Knock sets a loud knock audible from 300 feet.
You touch a creature and can end one disease or one condition afflicting it: Blinded, Deafened, Paralyzed, or Poisoned.

One creature or object weighing up to 500 pounds rises vertically, up to 20 feet, and remains suspended for the duration. A creature can make a CON save to resist. The target can move only by pushing against a solid object or by being pushed.

Describe or name a specific kind of beast or plant. Concentrating on the voice of nature in your surroundings, you learn the direction and distance to the closest creature or plant of that kind within 5 miles, if any are present.

Describe or name an object that is familiar to you. You sense the direction to the object's location, as long as that object is within 1,000 feet of you. If the object is in motion, you know the direction of its movement. The spell can locate a specific object known to you, as long as you have seen it up close—within 30 feet—at least once.
You touch a nonmagical weapon. Until the spell ends, that weapon becomes a magic weapon with a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls. At higher levels: +2 (4th) or +3 (6th).

Three illusory duplicates of yourself appear. When a creature targets you with an attack, roll a d6 to see if the attack hits a duplicate: 1-2 you're hit, 3-4 first duplicate, 5-6 second duplicate (if three exist). A hit destroys a duplicate.
Briefly surrounded by silvery mist, you teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

A silvery beam of pale light shines down in a 5-foot-radius, 40-foot-high cylinder centered on a point within range. Until the spell ends, dim light fills the cylinder. When a creature enters the spell's area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, it is engulfed in ghostly flames that cause searing pain, and it must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 2d10 radiant damage on a failed save.

A veil of shadows and silence radiates from you, masking you and your companions from detection. For the duration, each creature you choose within 30 feet of you has a +10 bonus to Stealth checks and can't be tracked except by magical means.

You craft an illusion that takes root in the mind of a creature that you can see within range. The target must make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failed save, you create a phantasmal object, creature, or other phenomenon of your choice. The target perceives it as real and must use its action to interact with the phantasm.
Up to five creatures of your choice within range each regain 2d8 + your spellcasting ability modifier HP. This spell has no effect on undead or constructs. At higher levels: +1d8 per slot above 2nd.

You touch a length of rope that is up to 60 feet long. One end of the rope then rises into the air until the whole rope hangs perpendicular to the ground. At the upper end of the rope, an invisible entrance opens to an extradimensional space that lasts until the spell ends. The space can hold up to eight Medium or smaller creatures.
For the duration, you see invisible creatures and objects as if they were visible, and you can see into the Ethereal Plane. Ethereal creatures and objects appear ghostly and translucent.

You weave together threads of shadow to create a sword of solidified gloom in your hand. This magic sword lasts until the spell ends. It counts as a simple melee weapon and deals 2d8 psychic damage on a hit. It has the finesse, light, and thrown properties. If you drop or throw the sword, it dissipates at the end of the turn.

A sudden loud ringing noise emanates from a point of your choice. Every creature within 10 feet must make a CON save or take 3d8 Thunder damage (half on success). Non-magical objects in the area automatically take this damage. At higher levels: +1d8 per slot above 2nd.

For the duration, no sound can be created within or pass through a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on a point you choose within range. Any creature or object entirely inside the sphere is immune to thunder damage and is Deafened. Casting a spell that includes a verbal component is impossible there.

Until the spell ends, one willing creature you touch gains the ability to move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and upside down along ceilings, while leaving its hands free. The target also gains a climbing speed equal to its walking speed.

The ground in a 20-foot radius centered on a point within range twists and sprouts hard spikes and thorns. The area becomes difficult terrain for the duration. When a creature moves into or within the area, it takes 2d4 piercing damage for every 5 feet it travels.

You create a floating, spectral weapon within range that lasts for the duration. When you cast the spell, you can make a melee spell attack against a creature within 5 feet of the weapon (1d8 + spellcasting modifier Force damage). As a Bonus Action each turn, you can move the weapon up to 20 feet and repeat the attack. At higher levels: +1d8 per two slot levels above 2nd.

You suggest a course of activity (no more than a few sentences) to a creature that can hear and understand you. The creature must succeed on a WIS save or pursue the course of action you described. The suggestion must be worded to sound reasonable.

This spell wards a willing creature you touch and creates a mystic connection. While the target is within 60 feet of you, it gains +1 AC and saving throws, and resistance to all damage. Each time it takes damage, you take the same amount. The spell ends if either of you drops to 0 HP or if you and the target are separated by more than 60 feet.

A strong wind (20 miles per hour) blows around you in a 10-foot radius and moves with you, remaining centered on you. The wind lasts for the spell's duration. The wind has the following effects: deafens you and other creatures in the area, extinguishes unprotected flames, disperses fog/mist/gas/vapors, grants you advantage on ranged weapon attack rolls, ranged weapon attacks against you have disadvantage.

You conjure a mass of thick, sticky webbing at a point of your choice within range, filling a 20-foot cube. Creatures in the webs must succeed on a DEX save or be Restrained. A creature can break free by using its action to make a STR check against your spell save DC.

This spell creates an undead servant. Choose a pile of bones or a corpse of a Medium or Small humanoid within range. Your spell imbues the target with a foul mimicry of life, raising it as an undead creature. The target becomes a skeleton if you chose bones or a zombie if you chose a corpse. On each of your turns, you can use a bonus action to mentally command any creature you made with this spell.

Healing energy radiates from you in an aura with a 30-foot radius. Until the spell ends, the aura moves with you, centered on you. You can use a bonus action to cause one creature in the aura (including you) to regain 2d6 hit points.

This spell bestows hope and vitality. Choose any number of creatures within range. For the duration, each target has advantage on Wisdom saving throws and death saving throws, and regains the maximum number of hit points possible from any healing.

You touch a creature and it must succeed on a WIS save or be cursed. Choose one of: Disadvantage on ability checks using a chosen ability score; Disadvantage on attacks against you; WIS save or waste action doing nothing; +1d8 Necrotic damage from your attacks. At higher levels: concentration extends (up to 10 min at 4th, 8 hours at 5th+, 24 hours at 7th+, until dispelled at 9th).

A storm cloud appears at a point 100 feet directly above you. Each turn you can use a Bonus Action to call a bolt of lightning from the cloud to a point below it, dealing 3d10 Lightning damage (DEX save halves). In stormy conditions, damage increases to 4d10. At higher levels: +1d10 per slot above 3rd.

You create an invisible sensor within range in a location familiar to you (a place you have visited or seen before) or in an obvious location that is unfamiliar to you. The sensor remains in place for the duration, and it can't be attacked or otherwise interacted with. When you cast the spell, you choose seeing or hearing. You can use the chosen sense through the sensor.

You attempt to interrupt a creature in the process of casting a spell. If the creature is casting a spell of 3rd level or lower, its spell fails. For higher level spells, make a spellcasting ability check (DC 10 + spell's level). At higher levels: automatically counters spells of equal or lower level.

You create 45 pounds of food and 30 gallons of water on the ground or in containers within range, enough to sustain up to fifteen humanoids or five steeds for 24 hours. The food is bland but nourishing, and spoils if uneaten after 24 hours. The water is clean and doesn't go bad.

A 60-foot-radius sphere of light spreads out from a point you choose within range. The sphere is bright light and sheds dim light for an additional 60 feet. If you chose a point on an object you are holding or one that isn't being worn or carried, the light shines from the object and moves with it. Darkness created by spells of 3rd level or lower is dispelled.

Choose one creature, object, or magical effect within range. Any spell of 3rd level or lower on the target ends. For each spell of 4th level or higher, make a spellcasting ability check (DC 10 + spell's level). At higher levels: automatically ends spells of equal or lower level.

You project a phantasmal image in a 30-foot cone. Each creature must make a WIS save or be Frightened of you and drop anything it is holding. While frightened, it must use the Dash action to move away from you. It can repeat the save at the end of each turn.

A bright streak flashes and blossoms with a low roar into a 20-foot-radius explosion of flame. Each creature in the area must make a DEX save. Failure: 8d6 Fire damage. Success: half. The fire spreads around corners and ignites flammable objects not being worn or carried. At higher levels: +1d6 per slot above 3rd.

You touch a willing creature. The target gains a flying speed of 60 feet for the duration. When the spell ends, the target falls if it is still aloft, unless it can stop the fall. At higher levels: +1 target per slot above 3rd.

You transform a willing creature you touch, along with everything it's wearing and carrying, into a misty cloud for the duration. The spell ends if the creature drops to 0 hit points. An incorporeal creature isn't affected. While in this form, the target's only method of movement is a flying speed of 10 feet. The target can enter and occupy the space of another creature.

When you cast this spell, you inscribe a glyph that harms other creatures, either upon a surface (such as a table or section of floor) or within an object that can be closed (such as a book, a scroll, or a treasure chest) to conceal the glyph. When the glyph is triggered, it erupts with magical energy in a 20-foot-radius sphere.

Choose a willing creature within range. Until the spell ends, the target's Speed is doubled, it gains +2 AC, has advantage on DEX saving throws, and gains an additional action on each of its turns (only for Attack once, Dash, Disengage, Hide, or Use Object). When the spell ends, the target can't move or take actions until after its next turn.

You open a gateway to the dark between the stars, a region infested with unknown horrors. A 20-foot-radius sphere of blackness and bitter cold appears centered on a point within range and lasts for the duration. This void is filled with a cacophony of soft whispers and slurping noises that can be heard up to 30 feet away.

You create a twisting pattern of colors that weaves through the air inside a 30-foot cube within range. The pattern appears for a moment and vanishes. Each creature in the area who sees the pattern must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature becomes charmed for the duration. While charmed by this spell, the creature is incapacitated.

A 10-foot-radius immobile dome of force springs into existence around and above you and remains stationary for the duration. The spell ends if you leave its area. Nine creatures of Medium size or smaller can fit inside the dome with you. The atmosphere inside the space is comfortable and dry, regardless of the weather outside.

A stroke of lightning forms a 5-foot-wide, 100-foot-long line of energy. Each creature in the line must make a DEX save. Failure: 8d6 Lightning damage. Success: half. The lightning ignites flammable objects and can ricochet off surfaces. At higher levels: +1d6 per slot above 3rd.

You create a 10-foot-radius, 20-foot-tall cylinder of magical energy centered on a point on the ground that you can see within range. Glowing runes appear wherever the cylinder intersects with the floor or another surface. Choose one or more of the following types of creatures: celestials, elementals, fey, fiends, or undead. The circle affects these creature types.

You create the image of an object, a creature, or some other visible phenomenon that is no larger than a 20-foot cube. The image appears at a spot you can see within range and lasts for the duration. The image seems completely real, including sounds, smells, and temperature appropriate to the thing depicted.

As you call out words of restoration, up to six creatures of your choice within range regain HP equal to 2d4 + your spellcasting ability modifier. This spell has no effect on undead or constructs. At higher levels: +1d4 per slot above 3rd.

You step into a stone object or surface large enough to fully contain your body, melding yourself and all the equipment you carry with the stone for the duration. You can't be perceived by normal senses. Small physical damage to the stone doesn't harm you, but its partial destruction or a change in its shape (to the extent that you no longer fit within it) expels you.

For the duration, you hide a target that you touch from divination magic. The target can be a willing creature or a place or an object no larger than 10 feet in any dimension. The target can't be targeted by any divination magic or perceived through magical scrying sensors.

This spell channels vitality into plants. If cast as an action, plants in a 100-foot radius are overgrown and become Difficult Terrain for 1 hour. If cast over 8 hours, farmland yields twice its normal crop for 1 year.
For the duration, the willing creature you touch has resistance to one damage type of your choice: acid, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder.

At your touch, all curses affecting one creature or object end. If the object is a cursed magic item, its curse remains, but the spell breaks its owner's attunement to the object so it can be removed or discarded.

You touch a creature that has died within the last minute. That creature returns to life with 1 hit point. This spell can't return to life a creature that has died of old age, nor can it restore any missing body parts.

You send a short message of 25 words or fewer to a creature you are familiar with. The creature hears the message in its mind and can reply in a brief message of 25 words or fewer. Sending succeeds even across other planes of existence, though the spell is blocked by Lead.

Until the spell ends, freezing rain and sleet fall in a 20-foot-tall cylinder with a 40-foot radius centered on a point you choose within range. The area is heavily obscured, and exposed flames in the area are doused. The ground in the area is covered with slick ice, making it difficult terrain.

You alter time around up to six creatures of your choice in a 40-foot cube. Each target must succeed on a WIS save. Failure: Speed halved, -2 AC and DEX saves, can't use reactions, can take only an action OR bonus action each turn, and can't cast a spell that takes 1 action (but can use a bonus action spell). It can repeat the save at the end of each turn.

You grant the semblance of life and intelligence to a corpse of your choice that has a mouth, allowing it to answer the questions you pose. The corpse knows only what it knew in life, and it can't answer questions about events after its death.

You imbue plants within 30 feet of you with limited sentience and animation, giving them the ability to communicate with you and follow your simple commands. You can question plants about events in the spell's area within the past day, gaining information about creatures that have passed, weather, and other circumstances.

Spectral creatures flit around you in a 15-foot radius. Choose good/neutral (radiant/slashing) or evil (necrotic/slashing). Enemies that enter or start their turn there make a WIS save. Failure: 3d8 damage. Success: half. Your speed is halved while active. At higher levels: +1d8 per slot above 3rd.

You create a 20-foot-radius sphere of yellow, nauseating gas centered on a point within range. The cloud spreads around corners, and its area is heavily obscured. The cloud lingers in the air for the duration. Each creature that starts its turn in the cloud must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or spend its action that turn retching and reeling.

This spell grants the creature you touch the ability to understand any spoken language it hears. Moreover, when the target speaks, any creature that knows at least one language and can hear the target understands what it says.

The touch of your shadow-wreathed hand can siphon life force from others. Make a melee spell attack against a creature. On a hit, deal 3d6 Necrotic damage and regain HP equal to half the damage dealt. Until the spell ends, you can make the attack again each turn as an action. At higher levels: +1d6 per slot above 3rd.
This spell grants up to ten willing creatures you can see within range the ability to breathe underwater until the spell ends. Affected creatures also retain their normal mode of respiration.

This spell grants the ability to move across any liquid surface—such as water, acid, mud, snow, quicksand, or lava—as if it were harmless solid ground (creatures crossing molten lava can still take damage from the heat). Up to ten willing creatures you can see within range gain this ability for the duration.

A wall of strong wind rises from the ground at a point you choose within range. You can make the wall up to 50 feet long, 15 feet high, and 1 foot thick. You can shape the wall in any way you choose as long as it makes one continuous path. The wall keeps fog, smoke, and other gases at bay. Small or smaller flying creatures or objects can't pass through the wall.

Attempt to send one creature you can see within range to another plane. The target must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be banished. If banished to a harmless demiplane, the creature is incapacitated; on a successful save, they return at the end of the spell. If they started on a different plane and the spell lasts the full duration, they don't return.

Necromantic energy washes over a creature of your choice. The target must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 8d8 necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success. Plants and undead are treated specially.

This spell assaults and twists creatures' minds. Each creature in a 10-foot-radius sphere must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be affected. Affected creatures can't take reactions and roll a d10 at the start of each turn to determine behavior.

You summon elementals of CR 2 or lower. Choose one of the following: one elemental of CR 2 or lower; two elementals of CR 1 or lower; four elementals of CR 1/2 or lower; or eight elementals of CR 1/4 or lower.

You summon fey creatures that appear in unoccupied spaces within range. Choose one of the following options for what appears: one fey creature of CR 2 or lower; two fey creatures of CR 1 or lower; four fey creatures of CR 1/2 or lower; or eight fey creatures of CR 1/4 or lower.

Until the spell ends, you control any freestanding water inside an area. Choose one of the following effects: Flood, Part Water, Redirect Flow, or Whirlpool. As an action, you can repeat the same effect or choose a different one.

You touch a creature and grant it a measure of protection from death. The first time the target would drop to 0 hit points as a result of taking damage, the target instead drops to 1 hit point, and the spell ends. Alternatively, if the target would be killed outright by a spell or other magical effect, the spell instead negates that effect.

You teleport yourself from your current location to any spot within range. You arrive at exactly the spot desired. It can be a place you can see, one you can visualize, or one you can describe. You can bring along objects up to your carrying capacity. You can also bring along one willing creature of your size or smaller.

Your magic and an offering put you in contact with a god or a god's servants. Ask a single question about a specific goal, event, or activity to occur within 7 days. The DM offers a truthful reply: a short phrase, a cryptic rhyme, or an omen. Multiple castings in a day reduce accuracy.

You attempt to beguile a beast that you can see within range. It must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you for the duration. While charmed, you have a telepathic link and can issue commands. Each time it takes damage, it makes a new saving throw.

Thin and wispy flames wreathe your body. They shed bright light in a 10-foot radius. You can choose warm or chill flames. Warm flames grant resistance to cold damage and deal 2d8 fire damage to attackers. Chill flames grant resistance to fire damage and deal 2d8 cold damage to attackers.

You touch a willing creature. For the duration, difficult terrain doesn't cost extra movement, and the target can't be paralyzed or restrained by spells or nonmagical means. It can also spend 5 feet of movement to escape from nonmagical restraints.

You transform up to ten centipedes, three wasps, five hornets, or one scorpion within range into giant versions of their natural forms for the duration. Use the stat blocks for giant centipede, giant wasp, or giant scorpion. You can command them with a bonus action.

You conjure a vine that sprouts from the ground in an unoccupied space. When you cast this spell, you can direct the vine to lash out at a creature within 30 feet of it. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be pulled up to 30 feet toward the vine.

You or a creature you touch becomes invisible until the spell ends. Anything the target is wearing or carrying is invisible as long as it is on the target's person. Unlike regular Invisibility, this spell doesn't end when the target attacks or casts a spell.

You make natural terrain in a 150-foot cube within range look, sound, and smell like some other sort of natural terrain. A creature can discern the illusion with a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC.

A hail of rock-hard ice pounds to the ground in a 20-foot-radius, 40-foot-high cylinder. Each creature in the cylinder must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 2d8 bludgeoning damage and 4d6 cold damage. On a success, half damage. The area is difficult terrain until the end of your next turn.
Describe or name a creature familiar to you. You sense the direction to the creature's location if it is within 1,000 feet and is not protected by a nondetection spell or similar magic.

You tap into the nightmares of a creature you can see and create an illusory manifestation of its deepest fears. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become frightened for the duration. While frightened, the target must take the Dash action and move away from you. At the end of each turn the target can make a Wisdom saving throw; on a success, the spell ends.

This spell transforms a creature that you can see within range into a new form. An unwilling creature must make a Wisdom saving throw to resist. The new form can be any beast whose challenge rating is equal to or less than the target's (or the target's level, if it has no CR).

You touch a stone object of Medium size or smaller or a section of stone no more than 5 feet in any dimension and form it into any shape that suits your purpose. For example, you could shape a large rock into a weapon, idol, or coffer, or make a small passage through a wall.
This spell turns the flesh of a willing creature you touch as hard as stone. Until the spell ends, the target has resistance to nonmagical bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

You create a wall of fire on a solid surface within range. The wall can be up to 60 feet long, 20 feet high, and 1 foot thick, or a ringed wall up to 20 feet in diameter, 20 feet high, and 1 foot thick. The wall is opaque and lasts for the duration. A creature that enters the wall for the first time or starts its turn there takes 5d8 fire damage.

Objects come to life at your command. Choose up to ten nonmagical objects within range that are not being worn or carried. Medium objects count as two objects, Large as four, Huge as eight. Each animated object uses the statistics for an Animated Object of the appropriate size.

A shimmering barrier extends out from you in a 10-foot radius. The barrier prevents beasts and other creatures from passing or reaching through it. An affected creature can cast spells or make attacks with ranged or reach weapons through the barrier.

After spending the casting time tracing magical pathways within a precious gemstone, you touch a Huge or smaller beast or plant. The target must have either no Intelligence score or an Intelligence of 3 or less. The target gains an Intelligence of 10, the ability to speak one language, and if it was a plant, mobility and senses.

You create a 20-foot-radius sphere of poisonous, yellow-green fog centered on a point you choose within range. The fog moves 10 feet away from you at the start of each of your turns, rolling along the ground. A creature that starts its turn in the cloud must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 5d8 poison damage on a fail, or half on a success.

You contact your deity or a divine proxy and ask up to three questions that can be answered with a yes or no. The DM answers each question honestly. The spell doesn't account for circumstances that might change the answer.

You briefly become one with nature and gain knowledge of the surrounding territory. Outdoors, the spell gives you knowledge of the land within 3 miles. Underground or in natural settings, 300 feet. You automatically learn facts about the terrain, plants, bodies of water, prevalence of beasts, and presence of powerful celestials, fey, or undead.

A blast of cold air erupts from your hands in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in the cone must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 8d8 cold damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success. Creatures killed by this spell become frozen statues until they thaw.

You call forth an elemental servant. The elemental appears in an unoccupied space within range. Choose its type: air elemental, earth elemental, fire elemental, or water elemental. If you lose concentration, the elemental doesn't disappear but you lose control of it.

You mentally contact a demigod, the spirit of a long-dead sage, or some other mysterious entity. You can ask up to five questions. You must make a DC 15 Intelligence saving throw or suffer psychic damage and be incapacitated. On a success, the entity answers each question with one word.

Your touch inflicts disease. Make a melee spell attack against a creature within your reach. On a hit, you afflict the creature with a disease of your choice from the list of diseases below. The target must make a Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns for 3 consecutive turns; after 3 failed saves, the disease lasts for 7 days.

You pull wisps of shadow material from the Shadowfell to create a nonliving object of vegetable matter up to 5 cubic feet in volume. The item lasts a duration based on material type (cloth: 1 day, wood: 8 hours, stone or crystal: 12 hours, precious metals: 1 hour, gems: 10 minutes, adamantine or mithral: 1 minute).

Shimmering energy surrounds and protects you from fey, undead, and creatures originating from beyond the Material Plane. Until the spell ends, celestials, elementals, fey, fiends, and undead have disadvantage on attack rolls against you. You can use your action to end the spell early and perform a special dismissal or breaking enchantment.

You attempt to beguile a humanoid you can see within range. It must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you. While charmed, you have a telepathic link with it. You can use that telepathic link to issue commands on your turn, directing it to take actions. Each time it takes damage, it makes a new Wisdom save.

This spell shapes a creature's dreams. Choose a creature known to you as the target. The target must be asleep. You or a willing creature you touch enters a trance state, acting as a messenger. The messenger appears in the target's dreams. If the target is hostile, you can deliver a nightmare that deals 3d6 psychic damage.

A vertical column of divine fire roars down from the heavens in a location you specify. Each creature in a 10-foot-radius, 40-foot-high cylinder centered on a point within range must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes 4d6 fire damage and 4d6 radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.

You place a magical command on a creature that you can see within range, forcing it to carry out some service or refrain from an action. If the creature disobeys, it takes 5d10 psychic damage each day. A remove curse, greater restoration, or wish spell ends this effect.

You imbue a creature you touch with positive energy to undo a debilitating effect. You can reduce the target's exhaustion level by one, or end one of the following: a charm or petrification, a curse, a reduction to ability score or hit points, or any one effect from a list of effects.

You touch a point and infuse an area around it with holy or unholy power. The area can have a radius up to 60 feet. Celestials, elementals, fey, fiends, and undead can't enter the area, and such creatures can't charm, frighten, or possess creatures within it. You can also choose an additional effect from a list.

Choose a creature that you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be paralyzed for the duration. This spell has no effect on undead. At the end of each of its turns, the target can make another Wisdom saving throw; on a success, the spell ends.

Swarming, biting locusts fill a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on a point you choose within range. The sphere spreads around corners. Any creature in the sphere when you cast the spell must make a Constitution saving throw. A creature takes 4d10 piercing damage on a failed save. Creatures that start their turn in the sphere must make the same save.

Name or describe a person, place, or object. The spell brings to your mind a brief summary of the significant lore about the thing you named. The lore might consist of current tales, forgotten stories, or even secret lore that has never been widely known.

A wave of healing energy washes out from a point of your choice within range. Choose up to six creatures in a 30-foot-radius sphere centered on that point. Each target regains hit points equal to 3d8 + your spellcasting ability modifier. This spell has no effect on undead or constructs.

You become invisible at the same time that an illusory double of you appears where you are standing. The double lasts for the duration, but the invisibility ends if you attack or cast a spell. You can use your action to move your illusory double up to twice your speed.

You attempt to reshape another creature's memories. One creature you can see must make a Wisdom saving throw. If it fails, it becomes charmed by you for the duration. While charmed, the target has a dazed look. You can alter a memory of an event that lasted up to 10 minutes and that occurred within the last 24 hours.

A passage appears at a point of your choice that you can see on a wooden, plaster, or stone surface (such as a wall, a ceiling, or a floor) within range, and lasts for the duration. You choose the opening's dimensions: up to 5 feet wide, 8 feet tall, and 20 feet deep.

With this spell, you attempt to bind a celestial, an elemental, a fey, or a fiend to your service. The creature must be within range for the entire casting. At the completion, the target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, it is bound to serve you for the duration.

You return a dead creature you touch to life, provided that it has been dead no longer than 10 days. If the creature's soul is both willing and at liberty to rejoin the body, the creature returns to life with 1 hit point. This spell also neutralizes any poisons and cures nonmagical diseases.

You touch a dead humanoid or a piece of a dead humanoid. Provided that the creature has been dead no longer than 10 days, the spell forms a new adult body for it and then calls the soul to enter that body. The DM rolls on a table to determine the new race.

You can see and hear a particular creature you choose that is on the same plane of existence. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw, modified by how well you know the target and the sort of physical connection you have to it. On a failed save, the spell creates an invisible sensor within 10 feet of the target.

This spell allows you to change the appearance of any number of creatures that you can see within range. You give each target you choose a new illusory appearance. An unwilling target can make a Charisma saving throw, and if it succeeds, the spell has no effect on it.

As you cast the spell, you draw a 10-foot-diameter circle on the ground inscribed with sigils that link your location to a permanent teleportation circle of your choice whose sigil sequence you know and that is on the same plane. A shimmering portal opens within the circle and remains open until the end of your next turn.

You gain the ability to enter a tree and move from inside it to inside another tree of the same kind within 500 feet. Once per round, using 5 feet of movement, you can pass into one tree and exit from the second tree by appearing in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the second tree.

An invisible wall of force springs into existence at a point you choose within range. The wall appears in any orientation you choose, as a horizontal or vertical barrier or at an angle. It can be free floating or resting on a solid surface. You can form it into a hemispherical dome or a sphere. The wall is immune to all damage and can't be dispelled.

A nonmagical wall of solid stone springs into existence at a point you choose within range. The wall is 6 inches thick and is composed of ten 10-foot-by-10-foot panels. Each panel must be contiguous with at least one other panel. If the wall cuts through a creature's space, the creature is pushed to one side.

You create linked teleportation portals that remain open for the duration. Choose two points on the ground that you can see, one within 10 feet of you and one within 500 feet of you. A circular portal 10 feet in diameter opens over each point. Any creature or object entering one portal exits from the other.

You create a vertical wall of whirling, razor-sharp blades made of magical energy. The wall appears within range and is 100 feet long, 20 feet high, and 5 feet thick. The wall is opaque. When a creature enters the wall's area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, the creature must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 6d10 slashing damage on a failed save.

You create a bolt of lightning that arcs toward a target of your choice that you can see within range. Three bolts then leap from that target to as many as three other targets, each of which must be within 30 feet of the first target. A target can be a creature or an object. Each target must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 10d8 lightning damage on a failed save, or half on a success.

A sphere of negative energy ripples out in a 60-foot-radius sphere from a point within range. Each creature in that area must make a Constitution saving throw. A target takes 8d6 necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success.

You summon a fey creature of CR 6 or lower, or a fey spirit that takes the form of a beast of CR 6 or lower. It appears in an unoccupied space that you can see within range. If you lose concentration, the creature doesn't disappear and you lose control of it.

Choose a spell of 5th level or lower that you can cast, that has a casting time of 1 action, and that can target you. Cast that contingent spell (without spending a spell slot), and name a trigger. The contingent spell is cast when the trigger occurs.

You can cast this spell only at night. Choose up to three corpses of Medium or Small humanoids within range. Each corpse becomes a ghoul under your control. On each of your turns, you can mentally command any creature you animaged with this spell.

A thin green ray springs from your pointing finger to a target you can see within range. The target can be a creature, an object, or a creation of magical force. A creature targeted must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 10d6+40 force damage on a fail. If this damage reduces it to 0 HP, it is disintegrated.

For the duration, your eyes become an inky void imbued with dread power. One creature of your choice within 60 feet of you that you can see must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be affected with one of the following until the spell ends: Asleep, Panicked, or Sickened.

This spell allows you to find the shortest, most direct physical route to a specific fixed location that you are familiar with on the same plane of existence. If you name a destination on another plane, in a moving location, or a destination that doesn't exist, the spell fails.

You attempt to turn one creature that you can see within range into stone. If the target's body is made of flesh, the creature must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it is restrained. While restrained this way, the target must repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns. On three failures, it is turned to stone permanently.

You create a ward against magical travel that protects up to 40,000 square feet of floor space to a height of 30 feet above the floor. For the duration, creatures can't teleport into the area or use portals to enter the area. The ward also prevents planar travel. Damage type determined by alignment.

An immobile, faintly shimmering barrier springs into existence in a 10-foot radius around you and remains for the duration. Any spell of 5th level or lower cast from outside the barrier can't affect creatures or objects within it, even if the spell is cast using a higher level slot.

You create a ward that protects up to 2,500 square feet of floor space. Multiple effects protect this area: magical fog, locked doors, magical darkness, false doors, and other obstacles. The whole warded area radiates magic.

You unleash a virulent disease on a creature within range. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 14d6 necrotic damage. The target's hit point maximum is reduced by the amount of necrotic damage taken until a long rest. This reduction can't reduce the target's maximum below 1.

Choose a creature you can see within range. A surge of positive energy washes through the creature, causing it to regain 70 hit points. This spell also ends blindness, deafness, and any diseases affecting the target. This spell has no effect on constructs or undead.

You bring forth a great feast, including magnificent food and drink. The feast takes 1 hour to consume and disappears at the end of that time, and the beneficial effects don't set in until this hour is over. Up to twelve creatures can partake of the feast. They gain immunity to poison and disease, Wisdom saving throw advantage, and 2d10 temporary HP.

Your body falls into a catatonic state as your soul leaves it and enters the container. While your soul inhabits the container, you can telepathically sense any humanoid within 100 feet. You can attempt to possess a humanoid's body by forcing a Charisma saving throw.

You suggest a course of activity (limited to a sentence or two) and magically influence up to twelve creatures of your choice that you can see within range, which can hear and understand you. Creatures that can't be charmed are immune. The suggestion must be worded so as to make the course of action sound reasonable.

Choose an area of terrain no larger than 40 feet on a side within range. You can reshape dirt, sand, or clay in the area in any manner you choose for the duration. You can raise or lower the area's elevation, create or fill in a trench, erect or flatten a wall, or form a pillar.

Choose one creature within range. The target begins a comic dance in place: shuffling, tapping its feet, and capering for the duration. Creatures immune to charm are unaffected. A dancing creature must use all its movement to dance, has disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws and attack rolls, and grants advantage to attackers.

You beseech an otherworldly entity for aid. The entity can be a good deity, evil deity, a powerful elemental, or another cosmic power. The DM chooses what form the ally takes. The ally might be a celestial, an elemental, or a fiend. You must negotiate with it for its service.

You create an illusion of an object, a creature, or some other visible phenomenon within range that activates when a specific condition occurs. A creature can detect the illusion by succeeding on an Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC.

A beam of brilliant light flashes out from your hand in a 5-foot-wide, 60-foot-long line. Each creature in the line must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 6d8 radiant damage and is blinded until your next turn. Undead and oozes have disadvantage. On a success, half damage.

This spell creates a magical link between a Large or larger inanimate plant within range and another plant, at any distance, on the same plane. Any creature can step into the target plant and exit from the destination plant by using 5 feet of movement.

You give the willing creature you touch the ability to see things as they actually are. For the duration, the creature has truesight, can see into the Ethereal Plane, and notices secret doors hidden by magic, all out to 120 feet.

You create a wall of ice on a solid surface within range. The wall is 1 foot thick and up to 30 feet long and 10 feet high, or it's a hemispherical dome up to 20 feet in diameter. The wall lasts for the duration. Each 10-foot section has AC 12 and 30 hit points. Creatures in the wall's space take 10d6 cold damage.

You create a wall of tough, pliable, tangled brush bristling with needle-sharp thorns. The wall appears within range on a solid surface and lasts for the duration. You choose to make the wall up to 60 feet long, 10 feet high, and 5 feet thick or a circle with a diameter of up to 20 feet. Creatures entering take 7d8 piercing damage.

You and up to ten willing creatures you can see within range assume a gaseous form for the duration, appearing as wisps of cloud. While in this cloud form, a creature has a flying speed of 300 feet and has resistance to damage from nonmagical weapons.

You and up to five willing creatures within 5 feet of you instantly teleport to a previously designated sanctuary. You establish the sanctuary by casting this spell in the location you want to designate, while attuned to a specific place of worship to your deity. After you cast the spell in the sanctuary, you and up to 5 willing creatures within 5 feet can teleport to it.

You summon a celestial of challenge rating 4 or lower, which appears in an unoccupied space that you can see within range. If you lose concentration, the celestial doesn't disappear; it acts according to its nature.

A beam of yellow light flashes from your pointing finger, then condenses to linger at a chosen point within range as a glowing bead for the duration. When the spell ends—either because your concentration is broken or because you decide to end it—the bead blossoms with a low roar into an explosion of flame that spreads around corners. Each creature in a 20-foot-radius sphere must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking at minimum 12d6 fire damage.

You utter a divine word, imbued with the power that shaped the world. Choose any number of creatures you can see within range. Each creature that can hear you must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, a creature suffers an effect based on its current hit points: 50 or fewer—deafened; 40 or fewer—blinded and deafened; 30 or fewer—stunned, blinded, deafened; 20 or fewer—killed instantly.

You step into the border regions of the Ethereal Plane, where you remain for the duration. For the duration, you can move in any direction—up, down, or sideways. If you move up or down, every foot of movement costs an extra foot. You can see and hear the Material Plane, but everything looks gray, and you can't see anything more than 60 feet away. You can only affect and be affected by other creatures on the Ethereal Plane.

You send negative energy coursing through a creature that you can see within range, causing it searing pain. The target must make a Constitution saving throw. It takes 7d8+30 necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. A humanoid killed by this spell rises at the start of your next turn as a zombie that is permanently under your command.

A storm made up of sheets of roaring flame appears in a location you choose within range. The area of the storm consists of up to ten 10-foot cubes, which you can arrange as you wish. Each cube must have at least one face adjacent to the face of another cube. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking 7d10 fire damage on a failed save, or half on a success.

An immobile, invisible, cube-shaped prison composed of magical force springs into existence around an area you choose within range. The prison can be a cage (bars 1/2 inch thick, 1/2 inch apart) or a box (solid walls). A creature inside the cage can't leave by nonmagical means. The cage lasts for the duration.

You make terrain in an area up to 1 mile square look, sound, smell, and even feel like some other sort of terrain. Open fields or a road could be made to resemble a swamp, hill, crevasse, or some other difficult or impassable terrain. A pond can be made to seem like a grassy meadow, a precipice like a gentle slope, or a rock-strewn gully like a wide and smooth road.

You conjure an extradimensional dwelling in range that lasts for the duration. You choose where its one entrance is located. The entrance shimmers faintly and is 5 feet wide and 10 feet tall. You and any creature you designate when you cast the spell can enter the extradimensional dwelling.

You create a sword-shaped plane of force that hovers within range. It lasts for the duration. When the sword appears, you make a melee spell attack against a target of your choice within 5 feet of the sword. On a hit, the target takes 3d10 force damage. Until the spell ends, you can use a bonus action on each of your turns to move the sword up to 20 feet and repeat the attack.

You and up to eight willing creatures who link hands in a circle are transported to a different plane of existence. You can specify a target destination in general terms, such as the City of Brass on the Elemental Plane of Fire or the palace of Dispater on the second level of the Nine Hells. Alternatively, you can use the spell as an attack to banish an unwilling creature.

Eight rays of light flash from your hand. Each ray is a different color and has a different power. Each creature in a 60-foot cone must make a Dexterity saving throw. For each target, roll a d8 to determine which color ray affects it: 1 red (10d6 fire), 2 orange (10d6 acid), 3 yellow (10d6 lightning), 4 green (death save), 5 blue (petrification), 6 indigo (restrained), 7 violet (blind), 8 special.

You create an illusory copy of yourself that lasts for the duration. The copy can appear at any location within range that you have seen before, regardless of intervening obstacles. You can use your action to move this illusory copy up to twice your speed, and make it gesture, speak, and behave in whatever way you choose.

You touch a creature and stimulate its natural healing ability. The target regains 4d8+15 hit points. For the duration of the spell, the target regains 1 hit point at the start of each of its turns. The target's severed body members (fingers, legs, tails, etc.) are restored after 2 minutes.

You touch a dead creature that has been dead for no more than a century, that didn't die of old age, and that isn't undead. If its soul is free and willing, the target returns to life with all its hit points. This spell also neutralizes any poisons and cures normal diseases affecting the creature when it died.

This spell reverses gravity in a 50-foot-radius, 100-foot-high cylinder centered on a point within range. All creatures and objects not somehow anchored to the ground in the area fall upward and reach the top of the area. A creature can make a Dexterity saving throw to grab onto a fixed object it can reach.

By means of this spell, a willing creature or an object can be hidden away, safe from detection for the duration. When you cast the spell and touch the target, it becomes invisible and can't be targeted by divination spells or perceived through scrying sensors. The spell puts the target into a state of suspended animation.

You shape an illusory duplicate of one beast or humanoid within range. The duplicate is a creature, partially real and formed from ice or snow, and it can take actions and otherwise be affected as a normal creature. It appears to be the same as the original, but it has half the creature's hit point maximum and is formed without any equipment. The simulacrum lacks the ability to learn or become more powerful.

When you cast this spell, you inscribe a harmful glyph on a surface. The glyph can cover an area no larger than 10 feet in diameter. When triggered, the symbol unleashes a magical effect. Choose from: Death, Discord, Fear, Hopelessness, Insanity, Pain, Sleep, or Stunning.

This spell instantly transports you and up to eight willing creatures of your choice that you can see within range, or a single object that you can see within range, to a destination you select. The destination can be anywhere on the same plane. Roll on the Teleport table to determine how accurate the teleportation is, based on familiarity.
